item.title: Tag system with cross-track linking
item.intent: Tags must work across all three content tracks so a tag like agent-security connects series installments, commentary posts, and KB entries on a single tag page. Visitors must be able to follow a topic across all tracks from one place.
item.description: Build a tag index page and individual tag pages that aggregate content from series, commentary, and architecture collections. Tags are already in frontmatter across all three collections — this feature surfaces them as navigable pages.
steps: Create src/pages/tags/index.astro — query all three content collections, extract unique tags, display with per-tag counts
Create src/pages/tags/[tag].astro — for a given tag, query all three collections, group results by track (series, commentary, architecture), render with links to each piece
Add tag links on content detail pages (series/[slug], commentary/[slug], architecture/[slug]) that link to /tags/[tag]
Ensure tag pages handle the case where a tag only appears in one or two tracks

acceptanceCriteria.text: /tags route lists all tags used across content tracks
Individual tag pages are generated for each tag
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && ls dist/tags/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | grep -q .

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tag pages contain links back to content entries
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && TAG_PAGE=$(ls dist/tags/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head -1) && test -n "$TAG_PAGE" && grep -qi 'href=' "$TAG_PAGE"
